title: Intracranial Pressure
body_html:
ICP Key Points

Cerebral perfusion pressure is calculated by subtracting the intracranial pressure from the mean arterial pressure.

CPP = MAP - ICP

Intracranial pressure is raised in:

  • Hypercapnia
  • Hypoxia
  • Low cerebral perfusion
  • Accelerated hypertension
  • Conditions affecting CSF circulation e.g. mass lesions, obstructive hydrocephalus, venous thrombosis and oedema (which may be secondary to other causes such as infart or infection)
